title: premna glaberrima
intro: Premna glaberrima, also known as the Indian lavender, is a plant species in the mint family that is native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Premna glaberrima include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Premna glaberrima is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can be used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
